description_long: This is a stone cross-shaped obelisk with intricate carvings and reliefs on its surface, standing upright on a rectangular plinth. The cross arms and shaft are adorned with swirling patterns and a central raised circular motif, giving it a Celtic or early Christian appearance. Below the cross arms, the shaft features a relief of an animal, possibly a horse or mythical creature, and additional geometric designs. The base is a simple, roughly hewn rectangular block, slightly wider than the shaft, providing stability and a sense of permanence. The stone surface appears weathered, with a matte, rough finish, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The overall color is a pale, off-white or light gray, typical of limestone or sandstone.
description: A weathered, cross-shaped stone obelisk with intricate carvings and a rectangular base, featuring Celtic-style motifs and reliefs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cross arms, central circular motif, animal relief, and detailed carvings on the shaft.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the cross and shaft, with visible relief depth and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cross and shaft, with less detailed carvings and a plain surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the cross and shaft, similar to view 1, with visible relief depth.
attribute_ornamentation: Elaborate swirling patterns and a central raised circular motif on the cross arms and shaft; animal relief and geometric designs below the cross arms.
attribute_base_design: Rectangular, roughly hewn stone block, slightly wider than the shaft, with a simple, unadorned surface providing stability.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is the uppermost part of the cross, flat and slightly rounded, with carved patterns continuing to the top edge.
